provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Tourism (Fees and Charges) Regulations, 2015.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context requires otherwise— “ Act ” means the Tourism Act; [Cap. 65] “ approved accommodation establishments ” means accommodation establishments that do not meet the minimum requirements of the set standards for classification and grading as prescribed in the Sixth Schedule; “ fees ” means the licence fee and other fees and charges charged under the Act ; “ licence ” means the licence issued under sections 21 and 31 of the Act ; “ not yet classified accommodation establishments ” means those accommodation establishments that meet the set standards for the classification and grading exercise but are yet to be officially classified and graded as prescribed in the Sixth Schedule; “ unclassifiable accommodation establishments ” means those accommodation establishments that have limited basic requirements and cannot be classified under the existing set standards of grading and classification prescribed in the Sixth Schedule. Part II – Classification of fees and charges

provisions.title: Licence fees
provisions.content: The licence fees for the classes specified in the First Schedule, shall be paid on an annual basis in the amount prescribed in the Second Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Fees for a temporary permit for an additional vehicle, overland trucks and special foreign air charter
provisions.content: The fees for temporary permit of an additional vehicle, overland tourist truck and special foreign tourist air charters shall be as prescribed in the Third Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Fees for grading or re-grading
provisions.content: The fees chargeable for grading or re-grading facilities shall be as set out in the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Fees for replacement of a registration certificate and licence
provisions.content: The fees for replacement of a lost or destroyed certificate of registration or licence shall be as set out in the Fifth Schedule to these Regulations. Part III – Offences and penalties

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: A person who contravenes any of the provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and shall be liable, on conviction: (a) to a fine of not less than one million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than six months; and (b) to pay any the outstanding fees .

provisions.title: Revocation of GN. No. 228 of 2009
provisions.content: The Tourism (Fees and Charges) Regulations are hereby revoked. First Schedule (Made under regulation 3) Classification of tourism businesses Class “A” Activities under class “A” include: 1. Tour Operator, 2. Safari Out fitting, 3. Photographic Safaris, 4. Hunting Safaris, 5. Air Charter Services ( Non schedule flight), 6. Hot Air Balloon Safaris, 7. Horse Riding, 8. Tourism Sea farries and Boat Safaris, 9. Tourism Adventure Sports (Big game fishing, Scuba Diving etc), 10. Tourism Recreation ( Theme parks Wildlife ranch, Wildlife farm, Zoo, Snake Parks etc), 11. Mountain Climbing or Trekking, 12. Car Rental or Hire, 13. Travel Agents, 14. Handling agents Class “B” Proffessional tourism activities under class B include 1. Professional hunting 2. Tour guiding 3. Professional safari or commercial photographing/filming/cinematography Class “C” Tourism accommodation facilities under class C include 1. Exclusive luxury accommodation facilities/services 2. Lodges 3. Tented camps 4. Mobile Camps 5. Town hotels 6. Vacation hotels 7. Villas, Cottages and Serviced Apartments 8. Motel 9. Not yet accommodation facilities 10. Approved accommodation facilities 11. Camping and Caravan Sites 12. Tourism Hostels 13. Tourism Home stay 14. Unclassifiable accommodation establishments Class “D” Business under class D include: 1. Curio shops. 2. Cultural Heritage centers 3. Cultural Tourism Enterprises or centres 4. Private Museums 5. Camping equipment hire and mountain climbing gears hire Class “E” Business under class E include 1. Hire of extra vehicles to carry out a special activity under the current licence. 2. Overland trucks 3. Special foreign air charter not licensed under the Act Second Schedule (Made under regulation 3 ) Tourism businesses and license fees according to share structure, star or grade Class “A” Business Temporary permit of additional vehicles 50.00 2. Permit for overland tourist trucks 200.00 3. Special foreign tourist air charters 200.00 Fourth Schedule (Made under regulations 5) Fees for grading or regrading of facilities (Citizen and non-citizen) Charges Fees in US$ (i) Fees for grading or re-grading 2,500.00 (ii) Fees for a plaque 1,000.00 Fees mentioned in (i) and (ii) in the fifth schedule shall be made upon application, transport together with accommodation for assessors shall be provided by the applicant. Fifth Schedule (Made under regulation 6 ) Fees for lost or destroyed certificate or licence Charges Fees in USD 1. Lost or destroyed certificate of registration 30.00 2. lost or destroyed License 30.00 Sixth Schedule (Made under regulation 2 ) Difference between categories of accommodation establishments Categories Basic / Minimum requirements Comments Star 1 Accommodation facilities that meet acceptable standards of quality and offer basic facilities without compromising cleanliness or guest security. Should have all the essential items as well as meet all the requirements as stipulated in the criteria for standardization of hotels, restaurants and other tourism facilities of East Africa. The facilities should have: Adequate natural ventilation, and/or sufficient mechanical air conditioning provided. The facility should be maintained to an average temperature range of 15-27 degree C, where applicable. Interior Décor should be modest, of good quality and functional, with harmony of colours and theme. Furniture and Equipment should be adequate, functional, comfortable and appropriate. All should be clean and of good quality, taking into consideration the needs of disabled/ handicapped persons. Floors, Walls and Ceilings, Woodwork and fittings should be of good quality materials and in good condition and good finish. Lighting should be adequate, natural and/or artificial, with level of artificial illumination controllable. Decorations should be modest of fine finish and well maintained. For hotel to score from three star to five star must have a suite, at least 50% of hotel rooms should have balconies. Star 2 Accommodation facilities with adequate standards of quality which provides a moderate range of facilities that are positioned to meet the needs of price conscious travelers. They have limited Services available, whereby upon request other services can be provided sometimes at a fee. Star 3 Accommodation facilities that deliver service standards at a broad range of facilities and/or amenities that exceed above average accommodation needs. This type of accommodation facility is of quality design, whereby physical attributes are typically fit for purpose to enhance the overall guest experience. Star 4 Accommodation facilities with a wide range of facilities and amenities complimented by superior service standards to satisfy varied personal needs and want of their guests, as well as deliver a superior accommodation experience. This type of facility offer better quality design that reflects a high level of comfort expected by guests. Star 5 Accommodation facilities that demonstrate excellence across all areas of operation. Guests will enjoy an extensive range of facilities and amenities and comprehensive or highly personalised service relevant to the star. These facilities will display excellent design quality and attention to detail. A five star hotel should have a higher percentage of professional and qualified management, supervisors and operative staff. Approved Location, Building, Front office, Lobby/lounge/public areas, Function rooms, Restaurant, Bar, Kitchen, Guest rooms, Guest bathroom, Suite, Hygiene and sanitation, Safety and security, Sundry services, Human resources, General. Should be met by 100% Unclassifiable Location: (Accessible), Building: (Not less than 10 rooms), Front office:(AT least a reception area), Restaurant/ Bar, Kitchen, A certain percentage of Guest rooms ensuite with the bathroom, Hygiene and sanitation: (water supply and water storage, sewerage system, proper refuse handling), Safety and security: (Fire protection, first aid and general security), Human resources:(qualification of Management staff, Staff uniform and Medical Examination), General: (Insurance Not yet classified Should have all the essential items as well as meet all the requirements as stipulated in the criteria for standardization of hotels, restaurants and other tourism facilities of East Africa. Should be met by 100% but have not been classified yet for different reasons (e.g new establishments, change of management, name e.t.c Guest Houses This is an establishment offering modest and limited accommodation and or catering facilities.
